name: ExtensionDeviceDispense_Status
title: R5: preparation | in-progress | cancelled | on-hold | completed | entered-in-error | stopped | declined | unknown (new)
purpose:
This extension is part of the cross-version definitions generated to enable use of the
element `DeviceDispense.status` as defined in FHIR R5
in FHIR R4.

The source element is defined as:
`DeviceDispense.status` 1..1 `code`

Across FHIR versions, the element set has been mapped as:
* R5 `DeviceDispense.status` 1..1 `code`

Following are the generation technical comments:
Element `DeviceDispense.status` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.status` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).

element.short: R5: preparation | in-progress | cancelled | on-hold | completed | entered-in-error | stopped | declined | unknown (new)
element.comment:
Element `DeviceDispense.status` is not mapped to FHIR R4, since FHIR R5 `DeviceDispense` is not mapped.
Element `DeviceDispense.status` has no mapping targets in FHIR R4. Typically, this is because the element has been added (is a new element).
This element is labeled as a modifier because the status contains codes that mark the resource as not currently valid.
element.isModifierReason: This element is labelled as a modifier because it is a status element that contains status entered-in-error which means that the resource should not be treated as valid

element.short: preparation | in-progress | cancelled | on-hold | completed | entered-in-error | stopped | declined | unknown
element.definition: A code specifying the state of the set of dispense events.
element.comment: This element is labeled as a modifier because the status contains codes that mark the resource as not currently valid.

binding.description: Describes the lifecycle of the dispense.
